task: I’m updating the safety rules for a hands-on class and want them to be practical without turning into a giant lecture. Can you help me draft a short checklist for tool use, supervision, and what to do if someone ignores a rule?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — a short, practical safety checklist can stay simple and still be enforceable.

## Tool Use
- [ ] Use only the assigned tool
- [ ] Wear required PPE
- [ ] Inspect the tool before use
- [ ] Use the tool only as demonstrated
- [ ] Stop if something feels unsafe

## Supervision
- [ ] Use tools only with instructor approval
- [ ] Ask before changing setup or settings
- [ ] Do not leave a running tool unattended
- [ ] Stay in assigned work area
- [ ] Follow the instructor’s direction immediately

## If someone ignores a rule
- [ ] Stop the task
- [ ] Make the area safe
- [ ] Correct the behavior
- [ ] Review the rule
- [ ] Remove tool access if needed
- [ ] Report repeated unsafe behavior

## Simple rule
**Safety rule ignored = stop work first, correct second, continue only if cleared.**

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page class safety sheet** with student sign-off lines.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short, practical, no fluff. The “stop work first, correct second” part is the right way to say it. I can use this.
